sprint: Traveller Unpublished-Work Notice + World Card Constellation Crossing-Lines Fix
task: "1st for a traveller who once start creating. we need to tell if they do not publish their creation might get lost. this will help us in keeping data clean. second the star marker grid needs rework. it cannot mark any constellation which has crossing lines. check the stars assigned to vihupapa. the constellation is in shape of number 4." Two independent fixes. (1) New js/travellerSaveNotice.js -- a small floating toast, deliberately NOT slotted into .workspace's own fixed calc(100vh - 64px) grid (this codebase has hit that exact class of height-overflow bug more than once when a new sibling was added there), shown only when ALL of: no Magic Card is active (a Traveller, not a Creator, who already has real Cloud Backup via their claimed card), the active project has at least one real page ('once start creating'), it hasn't already been published this tab session, and it wasn't dismissed this tab session (sessionStorage, so it reappears on a genuinely new session, matching the exact reappears-next-session convention the Traveller Privacy sprint's own CreatorProjectStore.clearAll() already established rather than a permanent one-time-forever dismiss). Reads 'You're creating as a Traveller -- publish your adventure (or claim your Magic Card) to make sure it's kept safe.' with a Publish Now button (clicks the real #publishBtn) and a dismiss. Wired via configure()+refresh() (js/app.js), refreshed from js/pageRuntime.js's own PageRuntime.notify() choke point (every meaningful Workspace mutation already calls this) and from js/magicCardUI.js's refreshHeaderBadge() (every moment a Traveller may have just become a Creator -- claim/rename/rehydrate/boot); js/publishStudio.js's _finalizePublish() calls TravellerSaveNotice.markPublished() the instant a real Publish succeeds, hiding it for the rest of the session. (2) Root-caused and fixed a real, reported bug in js/cardPlatform.js's and js/magicCard.js's shared CONSTELLATIONS table: every connecting-line drawer in this codebase (js/magicCardArt.js's drawBack, js/magicCardUI.js's _renderConstellation) draws a straight line between each consecutive pair of points in the array's own order, and CYGNUS's original order (Top, Center, Bottom, Left, Right) drew its last segment (Left-to-Right) straight back through the Center point a second time -- exactly the overlapping 'number 4'-looking defect reported on a real claimed card. Confirmed via a real segment-intersection algorithm (not just eyeballed) before touching anything: the other four constellations (Orion/Cassiopeia/Ursa Major/Lyra) were already crossing-free in their base order and stayed that way, only CYGNUS had a genuine defect. Fixed by reordering to Top, Center, Left, Bottom, Right -- traces the identical five points with zero self-crossing or vertex pass-through, verified (not just reasoned) to stay crossing-free across every one of _placeConstellation()'s 4-rotation x mirror-on/off combinations too, since a rotation/reflection/translation never changes whether two line segments cross. Both files kept in lockstep, per this codebase's own established duplication discipline for this exact pair of modules. Verified via a new Node geometry script (a real orientation+onSegment segment-intersection test, not assumed) confirming: the original CYGNUS order reproduces a real, detected crossing; every constellation in both its base order and all 8 rotate/mirror combinations is crossing-free after the fix; a live Playwright screenshot of MagicCardArt.drawBack() rendering the fixed CYGNUS pattern shows a clean, non-overlapping shape. A new 15-assertion Playwright suite for the notice (a fresh Traveller with zero pages sees nothing; it appears once a page exists; dismissing hides it and persists across an in-page reload but reappears on a genuinely new session; a claimed Creator never sees it even with real pages; markPublished() hides it) all pass, plus full regression across the existing Gateway/Magic-Card Playwright suite (gw_final_video_test.js 15/15, gw_returning_unified_test.js 18/18, gw_completion_check.js, gateway_skip_check.js, gw_myprojects_clear_check.js 5/5, gw_smoke_handoff.js 6/6, gw_decline_choice_test.js 11/11) and both goldenBuild.js suites (World Builder v1 and v2, unaffected -- neither fix touches Builder files) pass unchanged, zero console errors. No Theme Contract/Engine V2 Scene Model/Builder/Creator Governing Rules change; confined to a new js/travellerSaveNotice.js plus js/app.js, js/pageRuntime.js, js/magicCardUI.js, js/publishStudio.js, css/style.css, index.html, js/cardPlatform.js, and js/magicCard.js.
